findings.locus: §7.1 class 2 / §7.2 decision-consumed Evidence
findings.claim: §7.1 defines retention class 2 as 'Evidence in the SUPPORTED_BY closure of the reasoning trail of a session that PRODUCED a Solution which reached the selection-constituting `approved` gate outcome (DDR-002 §5)'. But DDR-002 §5 (fetched) fixes the `PRODUCED` edge as '0..*' and states 'a session may explore multiple candidate solutions or conclude none' — and selection is 'the approving (GateDecision)-[:DECIDED_ON {outcome: approved}]->(Solution) edge' where 'approved is the sole selection-constituting outcome'. §7.1's own next sentence then concedes 'Whether non-selected candidates'\'' reasoning trails warrant class-2 survival resolves with the survival-mechanism gap (§9).' A session that PRODUCED multiple candidate Solutions, only one of which reached `approved`, has SUPPORTED_BY closures for the non-selected candidates too; the class-2 boundary as stated captures only the selected candidate's trail, yet the reasoning that led to rejecting the others is the primary feedback-loop input (DDR-001 §Reasoning Graph, RejectedAlternative). The record leaves ambiguous whether the non-selected candidates' Evidence is class-2 or class-3, and §9 defers the very question §7.1 needed answered to make the class boundary total.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-003 §7.1 class-2 definition vs its own §9 deferral of non-selected-candidate survival; internal incompleteness of the retention-class partition

findings.locus: §6.2 re-admission / §6.1 remedy boundary
findings.claim: §6.2 states 'Un-retraction executes as re-promotion: a fresh forward candidate through the full EA gate, materializing a new active version via the supersession path (DDR-002 §6) — the replaceability-first test applied to the retraction itself (the valid fact is its own correct replacement).' But §6.1's retraction prong requires 'no correct replacement exists' — a fact is retracted only when nothing can succeed it. §6.2 then re-admits it 'via the supersession path' treating 'the valid fact is its own correct replacement.' A supersession requires a predecessor version node to supersede (DDR-002 §6 Option A: 'superseding creates a new retained node and marks the old superseded'). The retracted node was read-excluded via a `RETRACTS` edge, not superseded — it is not a live version in a supersession chain. Whether re-promotion supersedes the retracted node, supersedes nothing (fresh promotion), or interacts with the standing `RETRACTS` edge is unspecified; the claim that supersession applies contradicts §6.1's premise that retraction fired precisely because no replacement existed.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-003 §6.2 ('via the supersession path', 'the valid fact is its own correct replacement') vs §6.1 retraction prong ('no correct replacement exists') and DDR-002 §6 supersession requiring a superseded predecessor version

findings.locus: §4.3 retention ≥ lookback + §7.1 class 3
findings.claim: §4.3 commits 'Every §7.1 working-tier retention window must be greater than or equal to every detection lookback window that reads the retained data' and §7.1 class 3 carries 'per-role retention windows (one per RG role)'. But detection (§3.1) reads across RG roles for a single signal — e.g. override-recurrence traverses ReasoningProgress conclusions AND their RejectedAlternative overrides AND (per DDR-002 §5 PROPOSED_FROM) potentially Evidence. If ReasoningProgress and RejectedAlternative carry different per-role windows, a lookback reading a joined multi-role trail is bounded by the shortest window among the roles it traverses, not by any single role's window. §4.3's coherence rule is stated per-window against 'every detection lookback window that reads the retained data' but does not resolve which role's window governs when one lookback reads multiple roles; the record specifies per-role windows and a global lookback-coherence rule without reconciling the cross-role read case, leaving §4.3 unenforceable as written for multi-role signals.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-003 §4.3 (retention ≥ lookback, per-window) vs §7.1 class-3 per-role windows and §3.1 signals that traverse multiple RG roles

findings.locus: §1.3 roles / §5.2 lifecycle transitions
findings.claim: §1.3 states the distillation-reviewer role discharges 'Operational-plane entry (§5.1) and lifecycle transitions (§5.2)' and §5.2 states 'Resolving, superseding, or re-activating a distilled lesson is itself a distillation judgment under the same distillation-class review'. ADR-008 §2.2 (fetched), the cited upstream authority for the operational-distillation control, scopes that control to 'Substantive, evidence-visible human review of the distinct distilled judgment, bounded by distinctness' with accountability 'Per-distinct-lesson'. A resolve/supersede/archive transition of an existing lesson is a withdrawal-leg act; ADR-008 §2.4 governs withdrawal ('read-exclusion and retention-demotion') and §2.1 scopes the invariant to 'withdrawal (un-promotion / retraction)' as a distinct lifecycle stage. DDR-003 assigns lifecycle-transition accountability to the distillation-reviewer role, but ADR-008 does not explicitly home the Operational-plane *withdrawal* control on the distillation control — §2.2's control-type row addresses distillation entry, not the transition/withdrawal of an already-entered lesson. DDR-003 extends the distillation control to cover transitions without citing an ADR-008 locus that grants that scope.

findings.locus: §5.1 interim posture + §8 conformance item 2
findings.claim: DDR-003 commits that 'until that shape and the review surface land, no distillation write into the Operational plane is conforming' and enforces this only at 'ADR-008 §6's review checkpoint pending mechanization.' This is a hard-to-reverse posture that forecloses the Operational plane entirely for the platform's lifecycle until two forthcoming, unauthored designs land (the candidate shape via a future graph-schema amendment; the review surface via the detection-promotion service design). The record commits the platform to a position — the Operational plane is unwritable in a conforming way — whose reversibility depends on work not yet scheduled against any dependency. The justification offered ('no distillation process is built and no distillation traffic runs') establishes the cost is currently zero but does not establish the timing is proportionate: the record does not state whether the detection-promotion design is a near-term dependency or an open-ended forward item, leaving a lifecycle-spanning KG plane gated on unscoped work.

findings.locus: §1.3 Roles + Decision.3
findings.claim: Decision.3 and §1.3 rule that the distillation-reviewer and promotion-gate EA are 'distinct roles' but 'the EA holds both initially,' with the holder set widened by 'a role-assignment act under the platform's access model.' The record simultaneously asserts (§1.3) that 'a future non-human role-holder is an amendment question for ADR-008 §2.2.' This creates a governance-posture tension the record does not resolve: ADR-008 §2.2 fixes both controls as human review with a named human answerable, but DDR-003 routes role-holder *widening* to a routine access-model assignment act while routing role-holder *nature* to an ADR-008 amendment. Where the boundary sits between 'widening the human holder set' (assignment) and 'changing what may hold the role' (amendment) is exactly the surface that determines whether a future automation of distillation review requires upstream re-ratification or ships as a config change — and the record fixes the two poles without fixing the line between them.

findings.locus: §6.2 + §9 executed-retraction detection analogue
findings.claim: DDR-003 rules the executed-retraction detection analogue (the retraction-side mirror of the promoted-origin invariant) as 'deferred-with-trigger' — realization deferred to 'first real retraction instance.' This is a reversibility judgment that the deferral is safe because §6.2's forward-act-only rule makes the missing detection 'an audit-reconciliation concern rather than a consumption defect.' But the record admits (§6.4, §8 item 4) that by-construction safety is a *read-path* claim only, and that a contested executed retraction whose governing verdict has flipped to approving is undetectable audit-side until the analogue lands. Committing the platform to an audit-integrity gap for the entire pre-first-retraction lifecycle — while ADR-008 §5.3 already named this exact gap as a *risk* requiring tracking, not merely a deferral — is a posture that deserves its own justification proportionate to the cost of an unreconcilable governance ledger, which the record supplies only as 'non-load-bearing meanwhile.'

findings.locus: §7.1 retention classes + §7.2 survival exemptions + §4 parameter mechanism
findings.claim: DDR-003 defers every retention-window value (class 3 working-tier, one per RG role) to operational-onboarding calibration while committing two interim survival exemptions (decision-consumed Evidence, promotion-consumed substrate) that are 'held until their deferred survival mechanism lands.' The posture commits the platform to running the loop under interim exemptions of unbounded duration (every §7.2 exemption is 'reversible at zero traffic' only so long as traffic stays zero) — the record does not state what event forces the exemptions to resolve into a real mechanism, so the interim is a standing commitment with no defined exit, not a bounded bridge.

findings.locus: Decision block + §1.1 authority chain
findings.claim: I attacked the altitude boundary — whether DDR-003 pulls against ADR-008's ratified principle layer by re-deciding what ADR-008 already fixed. It holds: DDR-003 consistently cites ADR-008 §2.2/§2.3/§2.4/§2.5/§2.6 as upstream authority, authors only the policy layer ADR-008 §2.6 explicitly routes here (remedy boundary, diagnosis dimensions, roles, calibration, retention, audit), converts landed schema commitments to citations rather than re-rulings, and states the one premise ADR-008 superseded (the formerly-ungated distillation write becoming the reviewed distillation class). findings.locus: §6.1 remedy boundary + §6.2 re-admission
findings.claim: I attacked the remedy boundary for reversibility-proportionality — whether the retraction/supersession split commits a hard-to-reverse position without justification. It holds: the record makes supersession the default (reversible-by-lineage-retention), reserves retraction for the conjunctive should-never-have-been-promoted-AND-no-correct-replacement case, and rules re-admission as always a forward EA-gated act so that no ledger side-effect can silently re-admit ground truth. The reversibility is proportionate to risk — the irreversible-feeling act (retraction) is the narrow exception with a durable why-no-replacement record, and un-retraction has a defined forward path (re-promotion via the supersession path). The posture is internally coherent and correctly grounded in ADR-008 §2.6's replaceable-vs-retracted boundary.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.6 remedy boundary + §2.3 un-promotion authority; DDR-003 §6.1/§6.2 instantiates the boundary with a proportionate reversibility posture

findings.locus: §6.1 remedy boundary / Decision.7 vs §6.2 re-admission
findings.claim: §6.1's retraction prong requires 'no correct replacement exists' — retraction fires precisely when nothing can succeed the fact. §6.2 then states 'Un-retraction executes as re-promotion: a fresh forward candidate through the full EA gate, materializing a new active version via the supersession path (DDR-002 §6) — the replaceability-first test applied to the retraction itself (the valid fact is its own correct replacement).' The record decides two things that conflict: retraction presupposes no replacement, yet re-admission asserts 'the valid fact is its own correct replacement' and routes through the supersession path, which per DDR-002 §6 Option A requires a retained predecessor version node to mark superseded. The retracted node was read-excluded via a RETRACTS edge, not left as a live version in a supersession chain. The record commits a re-admission consequence its own remedy-boundary premise does not support, and does not declare how re-promotion interacts with the standing RETRACTS edge.

The record decides a retention-class partition that is not total: the Evidence of non-selected candidates is neither firmly assigned to class 2 nor to class 3, and the question the class-2 definition needed answered to make the partition complete is deferred. findings.locus: §4.3 retention ≥ lookback / Decision.5 vs §7.1 class-3 per-role windows and §3.1 signals
findings.claim: §4.3 decides a binding coherence rule: 'Every §7.1 working-tier retention window must be greater than or equal to every detection lookback window that reads the retained data,' validated at configuration time. §7.1 class 3 decides 'per-role retention windows (one per RG role).' §3.1 decides signals — e.g. override-recurrence — that traverse multiple RG roles for a single detection (ReasoningProgress conclusions, their RejectedAlternative overrides, and per DDR-002 §5 PROPOSED_FROM potentially Evidence). When one lookback reads a joined multi-role trail, the effective retention bound is the shortest window among the roles traversed, not any single role's window. The record decides both per-role windows and a per-window coherence rule but does not decide which role's window governs the multi-role read, leaving §4.3's binding rule underspecified for exactly the multi-role signals §3.1 commits.

findings.locus: §6.2 re-admission vs §6.1 retraction prong + DDR-002 §6 supersession
findings.claim: §6.2 states 'Un-retraction executes as re-promotion: a fresh forward candidate through the full EA gate, materializing a new active version via the supersession path (DDR-002 §6) — the replaceability-first test applied to the retraction itself (the valid fact is its own correct replacement).' §6.1's retraction prong fires only when 'no correct replacement exists' — retraction is the case where nothing can succeed the fact. DDR-002 §6 Option A supersession 'creates a new retained node and marks the old superseded' and re-points `rebind:current` edges — it requires a live predecessor version node in a supersession chain. But the retracted node was read-excluded via a `RETRACTS` edge (DDR-002 §5), not marked `superseded`; it is not a live version. The record does not specify whether re-promotion supersedes the retracted node, promotes fresh with no predecessor, or interacts with the standing `RETRACTS` edge — and 'via the supersession path' contradicts §6.1's own premise that retraction fired because no replacement existed.

findings.locus: §7.1 class 2 definition vs §7.1's own non-selected-candidate deferral
findings.claim: §7.1 defines retention class 2 as 'Evidence in the SUPPORTED_BY closure of the reasoning trail of a session that PRODUCED a Solution which reached the selection-constituting `approved` gate outcome (DDR-002 §5).' DDR-002 §5 fixes `PRODUCED` as '0..*' — a session may produce multiple candidate Solutions, only one of which reaches `approved`. §7.1's very next sentence concedes 'Whether non-selected candidates' reasoning trails warrant class-2 survival resolves with the survival-mechanism gap (§9).' The retention-class partition is therefore not total: for a multi-candidate session, the Evidence of non-selected candidates falls neither cleanly into class 2 (the definition captures only the approved candidate's trail) nor unambiguously into class 3, and the record defers the disposition the class boundary needed to be exhaustive. A retention-class model whose classes do not partition the space cannot be enforced against.

findings.locus: §6.2 re-admission forward-act rule vs DDR-002 §5 / §7 #25
findings.claim: Attacked whether a governing-verdict flip on an executed retraction could re-admit a fact by ledger side-effect, contradicting DDR-002's landed read-exclusion keying. On the read-discipline dimension it held: §6.2 keys read-exclusion 'on the executed RETRACTS edge exactly as landed (DDR-002 §5)' and rules that an append-only re-decision 'changes consumption nothing' — consistent with DDR-002 §5's retracted-node read-discipline (gateway-enforced, keyed on the EA-approved `RETRACTS` edge) and §7 #25's executed-proposal scoping. The consequence of the human-only fence on the loop's automation surface — where the assignment-vs-amendment line falls for automating the review workflow as distinct from the judgment — is a side effect of the role/detection decisions that the record leaves undeclared.

findings.locus: §6.2 re-admission 'via the supersession path' vs §6.1 retraction prong + DDR-002 §6
findings.claim: §6.2 states 'Un-retraction executes as re-promotion: a fresh forward candidate through the full EA gate, materializing a new active version via the supersession path (DDR-002 §6) — the replaceability-first test applied to the retraction itself (the valid fact is its own correct replacement).' §6.1 fires retraction only when 'no correct replacement exists.' DDR-002 §6 Option A supersession 'creates a new retained node and marks the old superseded' and re-points `rebind:current` edges — it requires a live retained predecessor version node in a supersession chain. But per DDR-002 §5 the retracted node was read-excluded via a `RETRACTS` edge, not marked `superseded`; it is not a live version in a supersession chain. The record specifies neither what the re-promotion supersedes, nor how the new active version interacts with the standing `RETRACTS` edge, while asserting supersession applies to a case §6.1 defined by the absence of any replacement.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-003 §6.2 ('via the supersession path', 'the valid fact is its own correct replacement') vs §6.1 retraction prong ('no correct replacement exists') and DDR-002 §6 supersession (requires a superseded predecessor version node) + DDR-002 §5 (retracted node read-excluded via RETRACTS edge, not superseded)

findings.locus: §7.1 retention class 2 definition vs its own §9 deferral
findings.claim: §7.1 defines retention class 2 as 'Evidence in the SUPPORTED_BY closure of the reasoning trail of a session that PRODUCED a Solution which reached the selection-constituting `approved` gate outcome (DDR-002 §5)', then in the same section concedes 'Whether non-selected candidates' reasoning trails warrant class-2 survival resolves with the survival-mechanism gap (§9).' DDR-002 §5 fixes `PRODUCED` as 0..* — a session may produce multiple candidate Solutions, only one of which reaches `approved`. For a multi-candidate session the Evidence of non-selected candidates is assigned to neither class 2 (the definition captures only the approved candidate's trail) nor unambiguously to class 3, so the class-2/class-3 partition is not total. A retention-class model whose membership is undecided for a live, DDR-002-permitted case cannot be applied as a total function against, and §4.3's retention≥lookback validation cannot be evaluated for a class whose membership is undecided.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-003 §7.1 class-2 definition vs its own §9 deferral of non-selected-candidate survival, against DDR-002 §5 `PRODUCED` 0..*

findings.locus: §4.3 retention ≥ lookback (per-window) vs §7.1 class-3 per-role windows + §3.1 multi-role signals
findings.claim: §4.3 binds 'Every §7.1 working-tier retention window must be greater than or equal to every detection lookback window that reads the retained data,' validated at configuration time with incoherent pairs rejected. §7.1 class 3 carries 'per-role retention windows (one per RG role).' §3.1's override-recurrence signal traverses a joined multi-role trail — ReasoningProgress conclusions and their RejectedAlternative overrides, and per DDR-002 §5 PROPOSED_FROM potentially Evidence. When one lookback reads across roles carrying different windows, the effective retention floor is the shortest window among the traversed roles, not any single role's window. §4.3 states the rule per-window against 'every detection lookback window that reads the retained data' but does not resolve which role's window governs the cross-role read, so the coherence validation is unspecified — and hence unenforceable as written — for exactly the multi-role signals §3.1 commits.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-003 §4.3 (retention ≥ lookback, stated per-window) vs §7.1 class-3 per-role windows and §3.1 signals traversing multiple RG roles

findings.locus: §5.2 lifecycle-transition accountability vs ADR-008 §2.2 / §2.4 scope
findings.claim: §1.3 and §5.2 assign lifecycle transitions — 'Resolving, superseding, or re-activating a distilled lesson' — to the distillation-reviewer role 'under the same distillation-class review.' ADR-008 §2.2's operational-distillation control-type row is scoped to 'Substantive, evidence-visible human review of the distinct distilled judgment, bounded by distinctness,' accountability 'Per-distinct-lesson' — a control on distillation entry. ADR-008 §2.4's Revalidation obligation does name a stale lesson's 'governed transition (a resolution or supersession, itself a distillation judgment under §2.2)', which grants the scope for the revalidation-triggered resolve/supersede — but §5.2 and §1.3 assign the distillation reviewer accountability for re-activation and for all lifecycle transitions generally, a broader scope than §2.4's revalidation-triggered transitions. Re-activation of an archived lesson is a re-entry act; ADR-008 §2.4 governs withdrawal/expiry, not re-entry, and no ADR-008 locus is cited that homes re-activation on the distillation control. The general lifecycle-transition assignment cites §2.4's revalidation clause for a scope wider than that clause grants.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.2 (operational-distillation control scoped to the distilled judgment's entry, per-distinct-lesson) and §2.4 (revalidation-triggered governed transition is a distillation judgment; withdrawal/expiry posture) — DDR-003 §5.2/§1.3 assign the broader lifecycle-transition and re-activation accountability without a granting ADR-008 locus for the re-activation case

findings.locus: §9 'discharging the graph schema's §2.4 routing at the policy layer' vs DDR-002 §2.4
findings.claim: §9's executed-retraction-detection-analogue gap entry states its ruling is 'discharging the graph schema's §2.4 routing at the policy layer (§6.2: forward-act-only re-admission; deferral with the first-real-retraction trigger).' DDR-002 §2.4 routes two items to this record: the un-retraction remedy (the remedy-boundary territory) and 'a standing detection analogue of #15 for executed retractions.' §6.2 rules the first (forward-act re-promotion) but §9 explicitly re-defers the detection analogue as realization-only. The word 'discharging' overstates: one of two routed items is discharged at the policy layer and the other's realization is re-deferred, so the routed obligation is partially met, not discharged — the entry's own follow-on text ('what remains is realization only') concedes the residual the 'discharging' framing understates.

findings.locus: §1.2 two-distinct-controls seam vs ADR-008 §2.2 per-source-class calibration
findings.claim: Attacked the two-controls seam for conformance to ADR-008 §2.2 — whether DDR-003 collapses the distillation-class review and the EA promotion gate into one control, or mis-maps either onto the wrong ADR-008 source class. It held: §1.2 maps 'derive' to ADR-008 §2.2's operational-distillation control (substantive, evidence-visible, per-distinct-lesson, distinctness-bounded — matching the ADR-008 row) and 'promote' to the promotion class's per-write EA gate (DDR-002 §2.4/§5, §7 #15), and correctly holds that a distillation-review approval 'makes a lesson consumable track-record and nothing more — Catalog/Standards entry still requires the EA gate.' The two failure modes stay distinct; neither control is conflated. The seam conforms to the cited authority.

findings.locus: §6.2 re-admission read-exclusion rule vs DDR-002 §5 / §7 #25
findings.claim: Attacked whether a governing-verdict flip on an executed retraction could re-admit a fact by ledger side-effect, contradicting DDR-002's landed read-exclusion keying. On the read-discipline dimension it held: §6.2 keys read-exclusion 'on the executed RETRACTS edge exactly as landed (DDR-002 §5)' and rules that an append-only re-decision 'changes consumption nothing' — consistent with DDR-002 §5's retracted-node read-discipline (gateway-enforced, keyed on the EA-approved RETRACTS edge) and §7 #25's executed-proposal scoping. findings.locus: §7.2 survival exemptions + §4 parameter mechanism
findings.claim: §7.2 commits two interim survival exemptions (decision-consumed Evidence; promotion-consumed substrate) 'held until their deferred survival mechanism lands,' where the decision-consumed survival mechanism is itself a named-but-unscheduled §9 graph-schema gap. The record states the exemptions are reversible at zero traffic but names no event that forces them to resolve into a real mechanism — the interim is a standing commitment with no defined exit, not a bounded bridge. Once traffic begins, the never-expiring exemption accumulates with every gated promotion; a commitment whose undoing-cost grows unboundedly requires justification proportionate to that cost, which the record supplies only as 'non-load-bearing meanwhile' and the zero-traffic floor — neither of which bounds the interim's duration or defines its exit.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.4 — 'the traceable rationale of a decision that consumed a ground-truth fact must survive that fact's later mutation'; DDR-003 §7.2 discharges this via an exemption whose resolution trigger is unstated

findings.locus: §6.4 hold-for-investigation + §6.4 standing-conflict visibility vs the platform-posture cost of a held-out KG
findings.claim: §6.4 rules that an unresolved cross-class scope conflict 'means the platform knowingly holds observed reality out of the KG — the fail-safe direction, but only while visible,' with open conflicts re-surfaced each detection cycle 'until ruled; no auto-timeout, no auto-precedence.' The hold-for-investigation disposition is 'explicitly non-terminal, named owner' but carries no bound on how long observed reality may be held out. This commits the platform to a position — CMDB-observed deployed reality can be indefinitely absent from the Knowledge Graph pending an EA resolution — whose reversibility is bounded only by human resolution cadence, with no escalation, staleness bound, or forced-disposition trigger. For a plane whose whole value is tracking deployed reality, an unbounded held-out interval is a standing posture cost the record surfaces but does not weigh.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.5 (cross-class precedence: withhold-and-escalate to human resolution, fail-safe to withhold) — the resolution step is committed to a human with no bound; DDR-003 §6.4 fixes the disposition space but leaves the held-out duration unbounded, a posture cost for a lifecycle-committed plane (ADR-001 §2.4)

findings.locus: §5.4 archival accountability-mode assertion vs the Rationale mechanism-now/values-held cut
findings.claim: §5.4 states archival after a reviewed terminal transition is 'a policy-scheduled act under per-policy accountability.' The per-policy (rather than per-write) accountability-mode assignment for archival is stated definitively, under the same n=0 empirical floor the Rationale acknowledges ('n=0 promotions, n=0 distillations, n=0 retractions') at which §4 explicitly holds its constants as contested. Whether archival is per-policy or per-write is an accountability-mode calibration whose driving instance is absent; the record does not mark it as a form-fixed/mode-held distinction the way §4 handles its parameters, so a mode assignment lacking a driving instance reads as settled rather than provisionally posture-fixed under the record's own mechanism-now/values-held cut.

findings.locus: §3.2 no-leak invariant vs DDR-001 Feedback-Loop Architecture action classes
findings.claim: Attacked the §3.2 no-leak invariant (source-quality signals never cross into promotion) against the landed feedback-loop action classes, to test whether the record over-claims a separation the data-path does not support. It held: DDR-001's Feedback-Loop Architecture names two action classes — knowledge promotion (EA-gated) and source-quality feedback (feeds ingestion scoring, no promotion) — and §3.2 restates exactly that boundary with a registration rule that a novel action class enters one of the two paths additively, and no bypass. The invariant faithfully instantiates the cited upstream split.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-003 §3.2 checked against DDR-001 Feedback-Loop Architecture (two action classes: promotion EA-gated / source-quality no-promotion) — no-leak invariant faithfully instantiates the upstream split
